perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement: (i) the term " Receiving Party " means the Party receiving Evaluation Material; (ii) the term " Furnishing Party " means the Party providing Evaluation Material or causing Evaluation Material to be provided
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement: (i) the term " Receiving Party " means the Party receiving Evaluation Material and intending to use it to directly compete with the Furnishing Party; (ii) the term " Furnishing Party " means the Party providing Evaluation Material or causing Evaluation Material to be provided
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Receiving Party" is modified to include the intent to compete with the "Furnishing Party". This contradicts Section 9(a) which states that the agreement shall not limit either Party's right to independently develop or acquire products. If a Receiving Party intends to directly compete by using Evaluation Material, it introduces an in-text contradiction that makes the enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) No License. Neither Party grants a license, by implication or otherwise, under any of its trade secrets or other intellectual property rights to the Receiving Party. The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party's right to independently develop or acquire products without use of the other Party's Evaluation Material. The Furnishing Party acknowledges that the Receiving Party may currently or in the future develop information internally, or receive information from other parties, that is similar to the Evaluation Material. Accordingly, nothing in this Agreement will be construed as a representation or agreement that the Receiving Party will not develop or have developed for it products, concepts, systems, or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Evaluation Material, provided that 4 the Receiving Party does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of "Receiving Party" now includes the intent to compete directly with the "Furnishing Party" using the Evaluation Material. This directly contradicts Section 9(a), which explicitly states that the agreement should not limit either party's right to independently develop or acquire products without use of the other party's Evaluation Material. The change introduces a conflicting scenario where the Receiving Party is simultaneously allowed to independently develop products and also intends to compete using the Furnishing Party's Evaluation Material, creating a clear and direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Evaluation Material. (a) Confidentiality of Evaluation Material. All Evaluation Material (i) shall be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and considering the Transaction; (ii) shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party; and (iii) shall be provided by the Receiving Party solely to those of its Representatives to whom disclosure is reasonably required to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation or consideration of the Transaction, it being the intention of the Parties to restrict the dissemination of Evaluation Material to as small a working group as practicable.
perturbation.changed_text: Evaluation Material. (a) Confidentiality of Evaluation Material. All Evaluation Material (i) may be used for the purpose of evaluating and considering other potential transactions beyond the scope of this agreement; (ii) should be kept confidential to the best efforts of the Receiving Party; and (iii) shall be provided by the Receiving Party to all of its Representatives regardless of their need to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation or consideration of the Transaction, to facilitate comprehensive review
perturbation.explanation: The definition of the permitted use of 'Evaluation Material' in Section 3(a) is changed to allow it to be used for other transactions and disseminated widely. This directly contradicts the original intent of strict confidentiality and limited dissemination. This contradicts the agreement's overall purpose to protect confidential information related to the specific transaction, potentially weakening the obligation to safeguard Evaluation Material within Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Confidentiality and Use of Evaluation Material. (a) Confidentiality of Evaluation Material. All Evaluation Material (i) may be used for the purpose of evaluating and considering other potential transactions beyond the scope of this agreement; (ii) should be kept confidential to the best efforts of the Receiving Party; and (iii) shall be provided by the Receiving Party to all of its Representatives regardless of their need to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation or consideration of the Transaction, to facilitate comprehensive review. The Parties agree that all Evaluation Material is and shall remain the property of the Furnishing Party. Before providing access to Evaluation Material to any Representative, the Receiving Party shall inform such Representative of the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material, and shall advise such Representative that, by accepting possession of or access to such information, such Representative is agreeing to be bound by this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of Evaluation Material's permitted use, allowing it for other transactions and broader dissemination. This directly contradicts the original intent, outlined in the Perturbation Explanation, of strict confidentiality and limited dissemination, weakening the obligation to safeguard Evaluation Material. This constitutes an in-text contradiction as it introduces an ambiguity regarding the scope and enforcement of confidentiality within the document. The Perturbation Type 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' accurately describes this conflict. The Contradicted Text is now in conflict with the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: At any time after termination of discussions by either Party with respect to the Transaction, upon the request of the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly (and in no event later than five business days after such request) (a) redeliver or cause to be redelivered to the Furnishing Party all copies of all Primary Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is in a visual or written format and erase or destroy all copies of all such Primary Evaluation Material which is stored in electronic format, and (b) destroy or cause to be destroyed all Derivative Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time after termination of discussions by either Party with respect to the Transaction, upon the request of the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly (and in no event later than thirty business days after such request) (a) retain all copies of all Primary Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is in a visual or written format and erase or destroy all copies of all such Primary Evaluation Material which is stored in electronic format, and (b) transfer all Derivative Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of Evaluation Material within five business days. The modified text instead requires the Receiving Party to retain Primary Evaluation Material for thirty business days, but transfer all Derivative Evaluation Material. This contradicts the original requirement and also creates uncertainty about ownership. It's now unclear what the receiving party is authorized to do with the derivative information if they are required to transfer it, in clear violation of section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Return and Destruction of Evaluation Material. At any time after termination of discussions by either Party with respect to the Transaction, upon the request of the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly (and in no event later than thirty business days after such request) (a) retain all copies of all Primary Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives which is in a visual or written format and erase or destroy all copies of all such Primary Evaluation Material which is stored in electronic format, and (b) transfer all Derivative Evaluation Material in the possession or control of the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives to the Receiving Party. Nothing herein shall obligate the Receiving Party to provide any Derivative Evaluation Material to the Furnishing Party. Notwithstanding the return, destruction or erasure of Evaluation Material hereunder, the Receiving Party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by their confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Receiving Party to return or destroy Evaluation Material within five business days, while the modified text dictates the Receiving Party must retain Primary Evaluation Material for thirty business days and transfer Derivative Evaluation Material. This directly contradicts the requirement in the contradicted text, as both sections now specify different actions to be taken with the Evaluation Material after termination of discussions. The contradicted text states requirements for 'Return and Destruction of Evaluation Material,' while the modified text indicates 'retention' and 'transfer,' creating a clear inconsistency and uncertainty about which obligation takes precedence, especially in relation to section 6. Also note that while the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, there is still a contradiction because the 'Original Text' was different, and it creates an inconsistency that way.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of any provision of this Agreement by it or any of its Representatives, and that in addition to all other remedies which either Party may have, each Party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that money damages would be a sufficient remedy for any breach of any provision of this Agreement by it or any of its Representatives, and that it's at the Furnishing Party's discretion to seek further remedies.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that money damages are insufficient, and the parties are entitled to specific performance. The modified text states that money damages would be a sufficient remedy. This creates a contradiction regarding the available remedies for a breach of contract. Section 7 is now contradictory by stating money damages are a sufficent remedy which directly contradicts the point of the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Remedies. Each Party agrees that money damages would be a sufficient remedy for any breach of any provision of this Agreement by it or any of its Representatives, and that it's at the Furnishing Party's discretion to seek further remedies. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder. In the event that either Party should institute proceedings to enforce any provision of this Agreement, the prevailing Party in such proceedings shall be entitled to recover all expenses relating to the enforcement of this Agreement, including reasonable attorneys' fees and costs, in addition to any other remedies.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that money damages are insufficient, while the changed text asserts they are sufficient. The contradicted text is the changed text, which means that the modification creates a direct contradiction within Section 7, where remedies are addressed, making it unclear whether specific performance is available or not. This ambiguity creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: CIDCO agrees to cease and terminate immediately, and to cause its Representatives to cease and terminate immediately, any negotiations (other than with EarthLink and its Representatives) with respect to any proposals relating to any business combination, tender offer or acquisition transaction involving the acquisition by any third party of all or any portion of the stock or assets of CIDCO (any such proposal being a " Third-Party Offer " and any such acquisition being a " Third-Party Acquisition ").
perturbation.changed_text: CIDCO agrees to cease and terminate immediately, and to cause its Representatives to cease and terminate immediately, any active negotiations (other than with EarthLink and its Representatives) with respect to any proposals relating to any business combination, tender offer or acquisition transaction involving the acquisition by any third party of all or any portion of the stock or assets of CIDCO (any such proposal being a " Third-Party Offer " and any such acquisition being a " Third-Party Acquisition ").
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'active' before negotiations, the text now implies that passive negotiations are allowed. This directly contradicts the original intention to cease any and all negotiations and creates an in-text contradiction. Section 8 is affected due to this insertion. It's unclear what qualifies as 'active' vs 'passive' and adds ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Communications. CIDCO recognizes EarthLink will incur substantial expenses in conducting a due diligence investigation of CIDCO, and in consideration for EarthLink's doing so, CIDCO agrees to cease and terminate immediately, and to cause its Representatives to cease and terminate immediately, any active negotiations (other than with EarthLink and its Representatives) with respect to any proposals relating to any business combination, tender offer or acquisition transaction involving the acquisition by any third party of all or any portion of the stock or assets of CIDCO (any such proposal being a " Third-Party Offer " and any such acquisition being a " Third-Party Acquisition "). In addition, CIDCO agrees that from the date of this Agreement until the earlier of (a) the date a definitive agreement for EarthLink's acquisition of CIDCO is executed, or (b) October 31, 2001 (as the same may be extended by mutual written agreement of the parties, the " Restricted Period "), CIDCO will not, and will not authorize or permit any of its Representatives, to directly or indirectly, solicit, consider or encourage any inquiries or proposals for (or which may reasonably be expected to lead to) any Third-Party Offer, or engage in discussions, conversations, negotiations or other communications with or provide any information to, or otherwise assist or cooperate with any person, entity or group in connection with any Third-Party Offer.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that CIDCO agrees to cease and terminate immediately any negotiations. The changed text adds 'active' before negotiations, implying that passive negotiations are allowed. This directly contradicts the intention to cease any and all negotiations. The contradicted text (Section 8) contains the same altered language as the Changed Text, but its purpose is undermined by the introduction of 'active,' making it unclear what level of negotiation is acceptable and thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the confidentiality of their discussions, negotiations and investigations concerning a Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents a portion of the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the confidentiality of their discussions, negotiations and investigations concerning a Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'the sole and entire agreement' to 'a portion of the entire agreement' implies there are other agreements governing the same subject matter, contradicting the intent of Section 9(b) and introducing uncertainty about which terms control. If there are other agreements, then those will also need to be examined, which is in direct contradiction with 9(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Entire Agreement. This Agreement represents a portion of the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the confidentiality of their discussions, negotiations and investigations concerning a Transaction. (c) Amendment and Waiver. This Agreement may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate written instrument duly signed and delivered by or on behalf of both Parties. (d) Severability. The invalidity or unenforceability of any provision of this Agreement shall not impair or affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision of this Agreement unless the enforcement of such provision in such circumstances would be inequitable. (e) No Obligation to Complete a Transaction. It is expressly understood that this Agreement is not intended to, and does not, constitute an agreement to consummate a Transaction, to conduct or continue negotiations with respect to a Transaction, or to enter into a definitive agreement with respect to a Transaction, and neither Party shall have any rights or obligations of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this Agreement or by virtue of any other written or oral expression by the Parties' respective Representatives unless and until a definitive agreement with respect to a Transaction is executed and delivered by both Parties, other than for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Both Parties further acknowledge and agree that each Party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to provide or not to provide Evaluation Material to the Receiving Party under this Agreement, to reject any and all proposals made by the other Party or any of its Representatives with regard to a Transaction, and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement' to 'This Agreement represents a portion of the entire agreement'. The contradicted text (b) states the same thing as the Changed Text, which is that 'This Agreement represents a portion of the entire agreement'. Because the changed text implies that there are other agreements governing the same subject matter, it contradicts itself (contradicted text), which would lead to uncertainty about which terms control. Therefore, the change creates a direct contradiction within the document.
